mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods following a 0.25% rate increase, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals extreme labor market tightness that forces the Federal Reserve to pause tightening, triggering a short-term equity rally over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are negative in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 3.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in retail sales growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation indicates demand destruction driven by cost pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS declines and CPIAUCSL rises, with a 60-day forward return direction of positive in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects strengthening corporate output capacity and rising earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where the federal funds rate plateaus while real GDP growth remains above 2% annually signals a soft landing scenario, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in >65% of instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening economic activity and deteriorating corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods following a 3-month payroll decline, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units signals renewed consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in >60% of months where HOUST crosses the 1.5 million threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Contracting industrial production alongside rising inflation during a stagflationary regime reflects supply-side bottlenecks that compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in >65% of stagflationary observations, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample validation.
